pattern: With at most 16 numbers the full 2^n subset space is small enough to enumerate directly, so compute every subset's OR and tally how many hit the array's maximum possible OR.
hints: Because n is at most 16, you can afford to look at literally every one of the 2^n non-empty subsets.
The maximum achievable OR is simply the OR of the entire array -- including more numbers in a subset can only add bits, never remove them.
Enumerate each subset as a bitmask over the indices, compute its OR by combining the chosen elements, and count how many masks reach that maximum OR value.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length of one, minimum value
maximum length of sixteen, all the same small value
disjoint powers of two, every non-empty subset gives a different OR
overlapping bit patterns require exact OR-maximum tracking
values at the maximum allowed constraint value

stress.description: random arrays at the real size and value limits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 16))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
